Learning Opportunities: ABB India offers a wealth of learning opportunities, from exposure to cutting-edge technologies in automation and electrification to insights into global business practices.

Professional Development: Interns receive training and mentorship from experienced professionals, helping to develop both technical skills and soft skills such as teamwork, communication, and problem-solving.

Innovative Environment: Working at ABB India allows interns to be part of an innovative environment where they can contribute to real projects and see the impact of their work on industry and society.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Competitive Environment: The competitive nature of the company means that interns need to be highly proactive and dedicated to stand out, which can be demanding and stressful.

Workload Pressure: Depending on the department and projects, interns might face high workloads and tight deadlines, which can be challenging to manage alongside learning and adapting to a new work environment.

Limited Scope in Some Roles: Some internship positions might have a limited scope of work, providing fewer opportunities to gain hands-on experience in diverse areas.

But, here is the catch, they have got a tool called Whizible for managing projects and they won't use Microsoft Project Server because it is too expensive. They have DOCUMENTUM for document management, HP Quality Center for test cases management, Merant tracker for bug management. But, they won't use Microsoft Project Server, because they want to give work for project managers by letting them use Whizible.

They have a STAGGERED attendance system. The core working hours is between 10 AM to 4 PM. Everyone is expected to be at office during these hours. And if you are not in OFFICE during these hours, they will give you a call to let you know that you are MISSED at office. 

The work culture is pathetic. Any attempt to help them by suggesting a new work culture is met with SCORN or ANGER. They will suppress everybody who is not abiding by their rules.

They have a DADA culture. Everybody should say YES-YES to their boss. Also, they have a BIG BOSS to whom all concerns should be reported. Failure to report concerns is considered a lack of respect for the BIG BOSS. The BIG BOSS is capable of screwing your career for good. So, suck up to all your bosses.
